type.description: Tunnel protocol used to connect to the remote peer.

peer.description: Remote peer this router builds a tunnel to.

address.description: Public address (IP or hostname) of the remote peer. Responder model: the remote peer dials in.

auth.description: IPsec authentication material for the tunnel.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Secret in the tenant namespace holding the pre-shared key (key `psk`). Takes precedence over `psk` when set.

psk.description: IPsec pre-shared key. Autogenerated and stored in a Secret when omitted. Mutually exclusive with `existingSecret`.

remoteCIDRs.description: Remote networks reachable over the tunnel. Must be disjoint from the cluster pod/service/join networks (validated by the controller).

staticRoutes.description: Optional extra static routes programmed on the router.

nextHop.description: Next-hop IP address for the destination.

bgp.description: Optional BGP peering over the tunnel. Disabled by default.

localASN.description: Local autonomous system number. Required when `enabled` is true; must be a valid ASN (1..4294967295).

remoteASN.description: Remote autonomous system number of the neighbor. Must be a valid ASN (1..4294967295).

resources.description: Explicit CPU and memory sizing for the router VM.

cpu.description: CPU topology cores allocated to the router VM. Whole cores only; a fractional quantity (e.g. "1500m", "0.5") is rejected at admission rather than silently truncated to zero.

memory.description: Memory (RAM) allocated to the router VM.

image.description: Boot-disk image source for the router VM.

enabled.description: Import the boot disk over HTTP from `url` instead of cloning the golden-image PVC.

name.description: Golden image name in cozy-public. Cloned as the boot disk from PVC vm-default-images-<name> unless `enabled` is true.

url.description: HTTP(S) URL of a VyOS qcow2/raw disk image. Used only when `enabled` is true.

managementCIDR.description: Source CIDR allowed to reach the VyOS management API (SSH 22, HTTPS 443) through the first-boot firewall. This value and the controller's --management-cidr flag (T05/T06) must agree: both default to the cluster pod CIDR (10.244.0.0/16, the kube-ovn default) and must be kept consistent. On a cluster with a non-default `networking.podCIDR`, set this (and the controller's managementCidr) to that pod CIDR, or the firewall will reject the real controller source. An empty value requires `allowOpenManagement=true` (fail-closed).

allowOpenManagement.description: Permit an empty `managementCIDR`, leaving the VyOS management API unrestricted (no first-boot firewall). Fail-closed by default: an empty `managementCIDR` with this false aborts rendering.

cloudInitSeed.description: Opaque seed mixed into the VM firmware UUID. Change it to force a first-boot cloud-init re-run; clear it to preserve an existing VM's UUID across re-renders.
